# **I. Overview** {#i.-overview}

## **1\. Code Packages** {#1.-code-packages}

The system follows a modular architecture to separate concerns between the user interface and server-side logic.

* Backend Code Package: Built with Node.js and Express.js, it is organized into modules such as auth for JWT management, payments for VNPay integration, orders for escrow logic, and chat for real-time WebSockets.  
* Frontend Code Package: Developed using React.js, the structure includes components for reusable UI elements, context for global state (Auth/Wallet), and services to encapsulate API calls to the backend.  
* Infrastructure: Data persistence is handled by MongoDB, accessed through a dedicated database layer in the backend.

### ***Backend Code Package*** {#backend-code-package}

![][image2]

### ***Frontend Code Package*** {#frontend-code-package}

*![][image3]*

***Package descriptions***

| No | Package | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | *auth* | *Authentication and authorization module handling user login, JWT tokens, and session management* |
| 02 | *users* | *User management module for user profiles, roles, and permissions* |
| 03 | *products* | *Product and category management module for catalog, pricing, and inventory* |
| 04 | *orders* | *Order processing module handling order creation, tracking, and status management* |
| 05 | *payments* | *Payment processing module with VNPay integration, wallet, and transaction management* |
| 06 | *chat* | *Real-time chat and messaging module using WebSocket for customer support* |
| 07 | *delivery* | *Delivery and shipping management module for tracking and logistics* |
| 08 | *reports* | *Analytics and reporting module for business insights and statistics* |
| 09 | *constants* | *Common constants and configuration values used across the application* |
| 10 | *middlewares* | *Express middleware functions for authentication, authorization, and error handling* |
| 11 | *utils* | *Utility and helper functions for JWT, logging, and common operations* |
| 12 | *config* | *System configuration for database, environment variables, and API settings* |
| 13 | *MongoDB* | *MongoDB database layer for data persistence and storage* |
| 14 | *admin* | *Admin dashboard module for system administration and EKYC verification* |
| 15 | *auth* | *Authentication UI module with login and registration pages* |
| 16 | *profile* | *User profile module for managing account information and settings* |
| 17 | *checkout* | *Checkout process module handling cart review, payment, and order confirmation* |
| 18 | *home* | *Homepage/landing page module displaying featured products and promotions* |
| 19 | *product* | *Product display module for browsing, searching, and viewing product details* |
| 20 | *layouts* | *Layout components module providing reusable page structures* |
| 21 | *routes* | *Routing configuration module defining application routes and navigation* |
| 22 | *components* | *Reusable UI components module with Button, Input, Modal, and other elements* |
| 23 | *context* | *React Context API module for global state management (AuthContext, CartContext)* |
| 24 | *hooks* | *Custom React hooks module for reusable logic (useAuth, useFetch)* |
| 25 | *services* | *API service layer module encapsulating HTTP requests to backend* |
| 26 | *assets* | *Static assets module containing images, icons, and stylesheets* |
| 27 | *API* | *API configuration module with Axios setup and request interceptors* |

## **2\. Database Design** {#2.-database-design}

### ***a. Database Schema*** {#a.-database-schema}

![][image4]

### ***b. Table Description*** {#b.-table-description}

| No | Table | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | users | Stores user account information, including authentication details (username, password hash), contact info (email, phone), and their role in the system (buyer, seller, admin). |
| 02 | addresses | Contains shipping and contact addresses associated with users, used for delivering orders. |
| 03 | wallets | Manages the digital wallet for each user, storing the current available balance for transactions. |
| 04 | transactions | Records the history of all financial movements related to a wallet, such as deposits, withdrawals, payments, and refunds. |
| 05 | categories | Defines the classification hierarchy used to organize products (e.g., Electronics, Fashion) to help users browse items. |
| 06 | products | Contains details of items listed for sale by sellers, including name, description, price, condition, and status. |
| 07 | product\_categories | A junction table that links products to categories, allowing a single product to belong to multiple categories (Many-to-Many relationship). |
| 08 | wishlists | Stores a list of products that users have saved or marked as favorites for future reference. |
| 09 | orders | Records the summary details of a purchase made by a buyer, including total amount, shipping fee, and current order status. |
| 10 | order\_items | detailed list of specific products and quantities included within a single order (handling the Many-to-Many relationship between Orders and Products). |
| 11 | deliveries | Tracks shipping information for an order, including the carrier name, tracking code, current location, and delivery status. |
| 12 | reviews | Stores feedback, ratings (1-5), and comments left by users regarding a specific order or product transaction. |
| 13 | conversations | Manages private chat channels connecting a buyer and a seller to discuss a specific product. |
| 14 | messages | Stores the individual text content, sender information, and timestamps of messages exchanged within a conversation. |
| 15 | reports | Records disputes, complaints, or violation reports submitted by users regarding other users, products, or orders. |
| 16 | dispute | Records formal complaints filed by a buyer regarding a specific order (e.g., damaged item, item not received). It stores the reason, photo evidence, and the investigation status to help moderators make a final financial decision (Refund to Buyer or Release to Seller). |

***b. Physical database***

	![][image5]

# **II. Code Designs** {#ii.-code-designs}

## **1\. Purchase and Payment**  {#1.-purchase-and-payment}

### ***a. Class Diagram*** {#a.-class-diagram}

![][image6]

### ***b. Class Specifications*** {#b.-class-specifications}

#### ***1\. TransactionController***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | sendPurchaseRequest | **Input:** req (contains listing ID, message), res (response). **Processing:** 1\. Get the userId from the login token (Buyer). 2\. Check if the message or listing ID is empty. 3\. Call transactionService.createRequest(...). **Output:** Returns a success message (JSON) or an error if data is missing.  |
| 02 | createOrder | **Input:** req (contains request ID, agreed price), res. **Processing:** 1\. Get the sellerId from the login token. 2\. Call transactionService.generateOrder(...) to create a formal invoice. **Output:** Returns the created Order object (including the calculated fee). |
| 03 | confirmPayment | **Input:** `req` (contains `orderId`), `res`. **Processing:** 1\. Get the `buyerId` from the login token. 2\. Call `transactionService.processPayment(...)`. 3\. If the service throws an error (e.g., "Not enough money"), send an error message. **Output:** Returns a success status (200 OK) if payment is complete. |

#### ***2\. TransactionService***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | generateOrder | **Input:** `sellerId`, `requestId`, `agreedPrice`. **Processing:** 1\. Find the purchase request details in the database. 2\. Verify if this Seller owns the product. 3\. Calculate the platform fee (e.g., 5% of the price). 4\. Calculate `totalToPay` \= `agreedPrice` \+ `fee`. 5\. Save the new Order with status "Pending Payment". **Output:** The saved `Order` object.  |
| 02 | processPayment | **Input:** `buyerId`, `orderId`. **Processing:** 1\. Get order details from the database. 2\. Get the buyer's wallet balance. 3\. **Check:** If `balance` \< `order.totalAmount`, stop and show error. 4\. **Transaction:** \- Subtract money from the Buyer's wallet. \- Add money to the Escrow (Holding) wallet. \- Update the Order status to "PAID". **Output:** The updated `Order` object. |
| 03 | calculatePlatformFee | **Input:** `amount` (Number). **Processing:** 1\. Multiply the amount by 0.05 (5%). 2\. Round the number if necessary. **Output:** The calculated fee value. |
| 04 | createRequest | **Input:** `userId`, `listingId`, `message`. **Processing:** 1\. Validate that the user is not buying their own product. 2\. Call `buyRequestRepo.create(...)` to save the request with status "Pending". 3\. Send a notification to the seller. **Output:** The created `BuyRequest` object. |

#### ***3\. WalletRepository***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | findByUserId | **Input:** `userId` (String). **Processing:** 1\. Search the `wallets` table/collection for this user ID. 2\. If not found, return null. **Output:** The `Wallet` object (containing current balance). |
| 02 | decrementBalance | **Input:** `userId`, `amount`. **Processing:** 1\. Run a database command to subtract the amount from the user's balance. **Output:** Returns `true` if successful, `false` if failed. |
| 03 | incrementBalance | **Input:** `userId`, `amount`. **Processing:** 1\. Run a database command to add the amount to the user's balance. **Output:** Returns `true` if successful |

#### ***4\. BuyRequestRepository***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | create | **Input:** `data` (Object containing buyerId, sellerId, listingId, message). **Processing:** 1\. Insert a new document into the `buy_requests` collection. 2\. Automatically set `createdAt` to the current time. **Output:** The saved object with a new `_id`. |
| 02 | findPendingBySeller | **Input:** `sellerId`. **Processing:** 1\. Search for requests where `sellerId` matches and `status` is "pending". 2\. Return the list so the seller can view and accept them. **Output:** A list of `BuyRequest` objects. |

#### ***5\. TransectionRepository***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | createHoldTransaction | **Input:** `orderId`, `amount`. **Processing:** 1\. Create a record in the `escrow_transactions` table. 2\. Mark the type as "HOLD" and status as "SECURE". 3\. This proves the system is holding the money safely. **Output:** The transaction ID. |
| 02 | releaseFunds | **Input:** `orderId`, `sellerId`. **Processing:** 1\. Mark the previous hold transaction as "RELEASED". 2\. (Usually called when the buyer confirms receipt of goods). **Output:** Returns `true` if successful. |

#### ***6\. OrderRepository***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | create | **Input:** `orderData` (Object with agreedPrice, fee, totalToPay). **Processing:** 1\. Save the new order details into the `orders` collection. 2\. Set the initial status to "AWAITING\_PAYMENT". **Output:** The created `Order` object. |
| 02 | findById | **Input:** `orderId`. **Processing:** 1\. Find the order by its unique ID. 2\. Return null if it doesn't exist. **Output:** The `Order` object.. |
| 03 | updateStatus | **Input:** `orderId`, `newStatus` (e.g., "PAID")**. Processing:** 1\. Update the `status` field of the specific order. 2\. Update `updatedAt` time. **Output:** Returns `true` if the update was successful. |

### ***c. Sequence Diagram*** {#c.-sequence-diagram}

## 

![][image7]

### ***d. Database Queries*** {#d.-database-queries}

#### ***Phase 1: Purchase Request Creation***

##### **Query 1: Create a new purchase request**

db.buy\_requests.insertOne({

  "listingId": ObjectId("\<listing\_id\>"),

  "buyerId": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>"),

  "sellerId": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>"),

  "message": "\<buyer\_message\>",

  "status": "pending",

  "createdAt": ISODate()

})

#### ***Phase 2: Seller Creates Order*** 

##### **Query 1: Retrieve pending requests for a specific Seller**

db.buy\_requests.find({

  "sellerId": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>"),

  "status": "pending"

})

##### **Query 2: Create a formal order with calculated fees**

db.orders.insertOne({

  "requestId": ObjectId("\<request\_id\>"),

  "buyerId": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>"),

  "sellerId": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>"),

  "agreedAmount": 1000000, 

  "platformFee": 50000,

  "totalToPay": 1050000,

  "status": "awaiting\_payment",

  "paymentStatus": "unpaid",

  "createdAt": ISODate()

})

#### ***Phase 3: Buyer Payment to Escrow***

##### **Query 1: Check order details and buyer wallet balance** 

db.orders.findOne({ "\_id": ObjectId("\<order\_id\>") })

db.wallets.findOne({ "userId": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>") })

##### **Query 2: Deduct total amount from Buyer’s wallet**

db.wallets.updateOne(

  { "userId": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>") },

  { "$inc": { "balance": \-1050000 } }

)

##### **Query 3: Record funds held in Escrow**

db.escrow\_holds.insertOne({

  "orderId": ObjectId("\<order\_id\>"),

  "amount": 1000000,

  "status": "held",

  "createdAt": ISODate()

})

##### **Query 4: Transfer service fee to Platform wallet** 

db.platform\_wallets.updateOne(

  { "name": "Main\_Platform\_Wallet" },

  { "$inc": { "totalRevenue": 50000 } }

)

##### **Query 5: Update final Order status**

db.orders.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<order\_id\>") },

  { 

    "$set": { 

      "status": "escrow\_held", 

      "paymentStatus": "paid" 

    } 

  }

)

## **2\. Dispute Management** {#2.-dispute-management}

### ***a. Class Diagram*** {#a.-class-diagram-1}

![][image8]

### ***b. Class Specifications*** {#b.-class-specifications-1}

#### ***1\. DisputeController***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | raiseDispute | **Input:** `req` (contains transactionId, reason, evidence images). **Processing:** 1\. Get `buyerId` from token. 2\. Call `disputeService.createDispute(...)`. **Output:** Returns "Dispute Created" message (201 Created). |
| 02 | getPendingDisputes | **Input:** `req` (Moderator request). **Processing:** 1\. Call `disputeService.getPendingList()`. **Output:** Returns a JSON list of open disputes. |
| 03 | resolveDispute | **Input:** `req` (contains disputeId, decision: "REFUND" or "RELEASE"). **Processing:** 1\. Call `disputeService.processResolution(...)`. **Output:** Returns success message and the final status |

#### ***2\. DisputeService***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | createDispute | **Input:** `buyerId`, `transactionId`, `reason`, `evidence`. **Processing:** 1\. Update Transaction status to "Disputed" (`transactionRepo`). 2\. Create a new Dispute record (`disputeRepo`). **Output:** The created Dispute object. |
| 02 | processResolution | **Input:** `disputeId`, `decision`. **Processing:** 1\. Fetch dispute details. 2\. **If Refund (Buyer wins):** Call `handleRefundToBuyer`. 3\. **If Release (Seller wins):** Call `handleReleaseToSeller`. 4\. Update Dispute status to "Resolved". **Output:** Success/Fail boolean. |
| 03 | handleRefundToBuyer | **Internal Logic:** 1\. Remove funds from Escrow (`escrowRepo.deleteHold`). 2\. Add funds back to Buyer (`walletRepo.incrementBalance`). 3\. Mark transaction as "Refunded". 4\. Penalize the Seller (`applyPenalty`). |
| 04 | applyPenalty | **Input:** `userId` . **Processing:** 1\. Increment violation count (`userRepo`). 2\. If violations \>= 3, suspend account for 30 days (`userRepo`). |

#### ***3\. UserRepository***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | incrementViolation | **Input:** `userId`. **Processing:** 1\. Database Query: `db.users.updateOne({_id: userId}, {$inc: {violation_count: 1}})`. **Output:** True if updated. |
| 02 | suspendUser | **Input:** `userId`, `suspensionDate`. **Processing:** 1\. Database Query: Check if `violation_count >= 3`. 2\. Set `is_suspended = true` and `suspended_until` date. **Output:** True if user is suspended. |

#### ***4\. EscrowRepository***

| No | Method | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | deleteHold | **Input:** `orderId`. **Processing:** 1\. Database Query: `db.escrow_holds.deleteOne({ orderId: ... })`. 2\. This effectively "releases" the money logic so it can be moved to a Wallet. **Output:** True if deleted. |

### ***c. Sequence Diagram*** {#c.-sequence-diagram-1}

## 

## 

![][image9]

### ***d. Database Queries*** {#d.-database-queries-1}

#### ***Phase 1: Buyer Raise Dispute***

##### **Query 1: Update Transaction Status**

db.transactions.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<transaction\_id\>") },

  { "$set": { "status": "disputed" } }

)

##### **Query 2: Create Dispute Record**

db.disputes.insertOne({

  "transactionId": ObjectId("\<transaction\_id\>"),

  "buyerId": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>"),

  "sellerId": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>"),

  "reason": "\<dispute\_reason\>",

  "evidenceImages": \["\<url1\>", "\<url2\>"\],

  "status": "pending",

  "createdAt": ISODate()

})

#### ***Phase 2: Moderator Investigation***

##### **Query 1: Fetch Pending Disputes**

db.disputes.find({ "status": "pending" })

#### ***Phase 3: Moderator Decision***

#### ***Path A: Refund to Buyer (Seller at Fault)***

##### **Query 1: Remove Escrow Hold**

db.escrow\_holds.deleteOne({ "orderId": ObjectId("\<order\_id\>") })

##### **Query 2: Refund Buyer's Wallet** 

db.wallets.updateOne(

  { "userId": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>") },

  { "$inc": { "balance": 1000000 } } 

)

##### **Query 3: Update Transaction & Dispute Status**

db.transactions.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<transaction\_id\>") },

  { "$set": { "status": "refunded" } }

)

db.disputes.updateOne(

  { "transactionId": ObjectId("\<transaction\_id\>") },

  { "$set": { "status": "resolved", "resolution": "refund", "resolvedAt": ISODate() } }

)

##### **Query 4: Penalty: Increment Seller Violations & Conditional Suspension** 

// Increment violation count

db.users.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>") },

  { "$inc": { "violation\_count": 1 } }

)

// Check if count \>= 3 (logic performed by backend), then execute suspension:

db.users.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>"), "violation\_count": { "$gte": 3 } },

  { 

    "$set": { 

      "is\_suspended": true, 

      "suspended\_until": new Date(new Date().getTime() \+ (30 \* 24 \* 60 \* 60 \* 1000)) 

    } 

  }

)

#### ***Path B: Release to Seller (Buyer at Fault)***

##### **Query 1: Remove Escrow Hold**

db.escrow\_holds.deleteOne({ "orderId": ObjectId("\<order\_id\>") })

##### **Query 2: Pay Seller's Wallet**

db.wallets.updateOne(

  { "userId": ObjectId("\<seller\_id\>") },

  { "$inc": { "balance": 1000000 } }

)

##### **Query 3: Update Transaction & Dispute Status**

db.transactions.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<transaction\_id\>") },

  { "$set": { "status": "completed" } }

)

db.disputes.updateOne(

  { "transactionId": ObjectId("\<transaction\_id\>") },

  { "$set": { "status": "resolved", "resolution": "release", "resolvedAt": ISODate() } }

)

##### **Query 4: Penalty: Increment Buyer Violations & Conditional Suspension**

db.users.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>") },

  { "$inc": { "violation\_count": 1 } }

)

// Suspension logic for Buyer:

db.users.updateOne(

  { "\_id": ObjectId("\<buyer\_id\>"), "violation\_count": { "$gte": 3 } },

  { 

    "$set": { 

      "is\_suspended": true, 

      "suspended\_until": new Date(new Date().getTime() \+ (30 \* 24 \* 60 \* 60 \* 1000)) 

    } 

  }

)
